---
title: "Epoch QA"
---

> **Status**: Verified against `dsa110_continuum/photometry/epoch_qa.py` and `batch_pipeline.py` (2026-03-09)
> **See also**: [Pipeline Overview](pipeline-overview.qmd) | [Outputs and Artifacts](outputs-and-artifacts.qmd)

## Purpose

Each hourly epoch mosaic must pass quality checks before its photometry is
trusted for multi-epoch light curve analysis. The epoch QA answers one question:

> **Is this epoch's flux scale, source recovery, and noise level good enough to
> produce reliable forced photometry?**

Epochs that fail QA are logged in `products/qa_summary.csv` but excluded from
downstream science products. This prevents corrupted calibration, excessive RFI,
or missing data from silently contaminating light curves.

---

## Three-gate structure

All non-SKIP gates must pass for the epoch to receive a QA-PASS verdict.

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
    MOSAIC[/"Epoch mosaic FITS"/]
    NVSS[("NVSS catalog\n≥ 50 mJy")]

    MOSAIC & NVSS --> M["measure_epoch_qa()"]

    M --> G1{"Flux scale\nratio in\n[0.8, 1.2]?"}
    M --> G2{"Completeness\n≥ 60%\nrecovered?"}
    M --> G3{"Noise floor\nRMS ≤ 17.1\nmJy/beam?"}

    G1 & G2 & G3 --> V{"All active\ngates PASS?"}

    V -->|Yes| PASS["QA-PASS"]
    V -->|No| FAIL["QA-FAIL"]

    PASS & FAIL --> OUT["qa_summary.csv +\ndiagnostic PNG"]

    style PASS fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#4ec9b0
    style FAIL fill:#4a2222,stroke:#e74c3c
```

---

## Gate details

### Gate 1: Flux scale

| | |
|-|-|
| **Metric** | Median of (DSA peak flux / NVSS catalog flux) across recovered sources |
| **Pass** | `0.8 ≤ median_ratio ≤ 1.2` and at least 3 detections |
| **Fail** | Ratio outside range, `NaN`, or fewer than 3 S/N ≥ 5 detections |
| **Catches** | Miscalibrated bandpass, wrong cal-table applied, cross-strip calibration mismatch |

*Observed failure mode*: Applying Dec +16° cal tables to a Dec +33° observation produces median ratio ≈ 0.06.

### Gate 2: Detection completeness

| | |
|-|-|
| **Metric** | Fraction of NVSS sources ≥ 50 mJy in the mosaic footprint recovered above 5σ local RMS |
| **Pass** | `completeness ≥ 60%` |
| **SKIP** | Fewer than 5 NVSS sources in footprint (avoids spurious failures on sparse fields) |
| **Fail** | Completeness below 60% with ≥ 5 catalog sources |
| **Catches** | Imaging failures, excessive flagging, phase corruption that smears sources below detection |

**Recovery definition**: A source is "recovered" when the peak pixel in a **3×3-pixel box** (`_peak_in_box(half=1)`) centered on the NVSS catalog position exceeds **5× the local RMS**. Local RMS is the MAD-based estimate (`1.4826 × median(|pixel − median|)`) computed in a **50×50-pixel box** with the **central 10×10 pixels masked** (`_local_rms(outer=25, inner=5)`).

::: {.callout-note}
This recovery definition uses a smaller box than the forced photometry measurement in `batch_pipeline.py` (`measure_forced_peak`, box_size_pix=5). The QA gate intentionally uses a tight box to detect only genuine point-source matches.
:::

### Gate 3: Noise floor

| | |
|-|-|
| **Metric** | Global MAD-based RMS of the full mosaic (mJy/beam) |
| **Pass** | `RMS ≤ 17.1 mJy/beam` |
| **Fail** | RMS exceeds limit |
| **Catches** | Severe RFI contamination, calibration divergence, corrupt data |

::: {.callout-warning}
**Current limitation**: The 17.1 mJy threshold is 2× the empirical floor of 8.54 mJy/beam (validated on 2026-01-25 22h epoch with BP-only fallback calibration). The theoretical thermal noise from SEFD/Tsys has not been measured for DSA-110. The code marks this with `# TODO: recompute when Tsys/SEFD is measured`. The threshold will be updated when `dsa110_measured_parameters.yaml` contains measured values.
:::

---

## The canary tile

The **canary tile** is a single pre-existing tile image used for fast regression
testing without reprocessing:

- **Tile**: `2026-01-25T22:26:05` (contains 3C454.3 at ~12.5 Jy/beam)
- **Script**: `scripts/run_canary.sh`
- **Runtime**: < 1 minute (reads pre-existing tile FITS, does not re-run calibration or imaging)
- **Acceptance criteria** (looser than full epoch QA gates):
  - `median_ratio` ∈ [0.85, 1.15]
  - `n_recovered ≥ 3`
  - `RMS ≤ 17.1 mJy/beam`

The canary runs `measure_epoch_qa()` directly on the tile image (not on an epoch
mosaic), so it tests the same code path used by `batch_pipeline.py`, without the
~3 hour cost of a full 11-tile epoch run.

---

## Artifacts produced

Both QA-PASS and QA-FAIL epochs produce the same artifacts (so failures are
always auditable):

| Artifact | Path | Purpose |
|----------|------|---------|
| QA summary row | `products/qa_summary.csv` (append) | Machine-readable log of all epoch verdicts |
| Diagnostic PNG | `{stage_dir}/{date}T{HH}00_mosaic_qa_diag.png` | Three-panel visual verdict |

### Diagnostic PNG layout

Three-panel figure (14×4 inches, dark theme):

1. **Left**: DSA/NVSS ratio histogram with [0.8, 1.2] acceptance band shaded and median marked
2. **Center**: Detection completeness bar (% recovered) with 60% threshold line
3. **Right**: Per-tile RMS bar chart with 17.1 mJy/beam limit line; bars colored green/red per limit

Title shows epoch label and overall verdict: **PASS** (green) or **FAIL** (red).

---

## How epoch QA fits into the batch pipeline

In `batch_pipeline.py`, for each 1-hour epoch:

1. Tiles are mosaicked via `build_common_wcs()` + `coadd_tiles()`
2. Basic noise-strip QA runs (`check_mosaic_quality()` — legacy, max/min strip noise ratio < 3.0)
3. Forced photometry runs at NVSS positions ≥ 10 mJy (`run_photometry_phase()`)
4. Three-gate epoch QA runs independently (`measure_epoch_qa()`, NVSS ≥ 50 mJy)
5. Diagnostic PNG is written (`plot_epoch_qa()`)
6. A row is appended to `products/qa_summary.csv`
7. The epoch mosaic FITS is copied to `products/mosaics/{date}/`

Steps 3 and 4 are independent — forced photometry does not depend on the QA verdict,
and the QA gate does not use the forced photometry output.

---

## Implementation status

| Component | Status |
|-----------|--------|
| `epoch_qa.py` — `measure_epoch_qa()` | *Verified* — unit tested (5 tests in `tests/test_epoch_qa.py`) |
| `epoch_qa_plot.py` — `plot_epoch_qa()` | *Verified* — unit tested (2 tests in `tests/test_epoch_qa_plot.py`) |
| Wiring into `batch_pipeline.py` | *Verified* — tested on 2026-01-25; not yet run at scale |
| `run_canary.sh` | *Verified* |
| `qa_summary.csv` schema migration | *Verified* — stale CSV auto-archived on schema mismatch |
| Noise threshold anchored to Tsys/SEFD | *Planned* — currently empirical |
| Per-tile QA (tile rejection before mosaicking) | *Planned* — currently only epoch-level |

---

## Constants reference

All constants are defined in `dsa110_continuum/photometry/epoch_qa.py`.

| Constant | Value | Notes |
|----------|-------|-------|
| `QA_RATIO_LOW` | 0.8 | Lower bound of flux-scale acceptance |
| `QA_RATIO_HIGH` | 1.2 | Upper bound of flux-scale acceptance |
| `QA_COMPLETENESS_MIN` | 0.60 | Minimum fraction of catalog sources recovered |
| `QA_MIN_CATALOG_SOURCES` | 5 | Below this, completeness gate = SKIP |
| `QA_MIN_FLUX_MJY` | 50.0 | NVSS flux threshold for QA catalog query |
| `QA_RECOVERY_SIGMA` | 5.0 | Local-RMS multiple for "recovered" |
| `QA_MIN_RATIO_DETECTIONS` | 3 | Minimum detections for flux-scale gate |
| `QA_RMS_LIMIT_MJY` | 17.1 | 2× empirical floor (8.54 mJy, Jan 25 22h) |
